title: Continuous Batching & Speculative Decoding
learning_outcomes: Formulate Speculative Decoding draft-target verification steps.

content_sections.title: Continuous Batching (Iteration-Level Scheduling)
content_sections.content:
Static batching requires all sequences in a batch to finish generation before processing a new batch, causing compute idle time due to sequence length variance. 

**Continuous Batching** schedules tasks at the iteration level, inserting new requests as soon as a sequence completes. This minimizes idle resources and maximizes GPU utilization.

content_sections.title: Speculative Decoding Mathematics
content_sections.content:
**Speculative Decoding** accelerates generation without losing accuracy by using a small draft model to speculate $K$ future tokens. The target model validates them in parallel in a single forward pass.

A speculated token $x$ is accepted with probability:

$$P_{accept} = \min\left(1.0, \frac{P_{target}(x \mid context)}{P_{draft}(x \mid context)}\right)$$

If the token is rejected, we sample a replacement token from the normalized difference distribution: $\text{softmax}(\max(0, P_{target}(x) - P_{draft}(x)))$, guaranteeing that outputs are mathematically identical to the target model.
